message:
refactor(fp-io): thread quote_identifier trait dispatch through SQL helpers (br-cx2x / fd90.12)

Closes br-frankenpandas-cx2x (fd90.12). Phase 2 of the fd90.10 trait abstraction: migrates the SQL helper functions that previously called the free quote_sql_ident free fn over to conn.quote_identifier(...) trait dispatch. Custom backend overrides (e.g. MySQL backtick quoting) now actually take effect on read_sql_table / write_sql / read_sql_chunks paths.

Helpers updated to take a &C: SqlConnection generic parameter:
- sql_select_all_query — read_sql_table family.
- sql_select_columns_query — read_sql_table_columns family.
- sql_column_definition — write_sql CREATE TABLE column-def construction.
- sql_create_table_query — write_sql CREATE TABLE statement.
sql_insert_rows_query already had the generic; switched its internal quote_sql_ident calls to conn.quote_identifier.

Behavior is unchanged for SQLite (rusqlite override delegates to quote_sql_ident). Verified by:
- All 256 prior tests pass unchanged (existing SQLite write_sql / read_sql_table round-trip behavior preserved).
- New test sql_query_builders_use_backend_quote_identifier_override constructs a BacktickSqlConn that overrides quote_identifier to MySQL-style backticks and asserts CREATE / SELECT / projection / INSERT statements all produce backticked identifiers without any further plumbing.

rch: 257 fp-io tests pass (256 prior + 1 new), cargo check --workspace --all-targets clean, cargo clippy -p fp-io --all-targets -D warnings clean, ubs Critical=0 exit 0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
